items: No production hosting is included in this generated output.
No public deployment is recorded by Public Alpha Rehearsal Evidence v0.
The LIVE_ALPHA_01 public-alpha wrapper is a local entrypoint and config guard only; it is not hosting approval.
Public Publication Plane Contracts v0 define route, data, client, and deployment-target governance; the static generator consumes those contracts without changing deployment.
GitHub Pages Deployment Enablement v0 configures static artifact publishing only; it does not host the Python backend or prove deployment success without GitHub Actions evidence.
Static Site Generation Migration v0 generates site/dist for validation, while site/dist remains the deployable artifact.
Lite/Text/Files Seed Surfaces v0 adds static compatibility seed surfaces only; it does not add live search, executable downloads, snapshots, relay behavior, or native-client runtime behavior.
Static Resolver Demo Snapshots v0 adds fixture-backed static demo pages only; it does not add live search, a live API, backend hosting, external observations, or production behavior.

Custom Domain / Alternate Host Readiness v0 adds readiness inventory, base-path policy, and operator checklist only; it does not configure DNS, add CNAME, deploy alternate hosts, add provider config, or claim production readiness.
Live Backend Handoff Contract v0 reserves /api/v1 route families only; it does not make /api/v1 live, deploy a backend, enable live probes, or create stable API guarantees.
Live Probe Gateway Contract v0 defines source policy, caps, cache/evidence requirements, and operator gates only; it does not implement live probes or make network calls.

Compatibility Surface Strategy v0 defines multi-surface policy, capability matrices, and route/profile alignment only; it does not implement snapshots, relay behavior, native clients, a rich app surface, live API behavior, or new runtime product behavior.

Public Search Safety / Abuse Guard v0 is policy-only; it defines local_index_only limits, forbidden parameters, privacy defaults, operator controls, and readiness gates but does not implement public search runtime, rate-limit middleware, auth/accounts, telemetry runtime, live probes, downloads, uploads, local path search, arbitrary URL fetch, or production safety guarantees.

Public Search API Contract v0 and Public Search Result Card Contract v0 are contract-only; they do not make /search or /api/v1/search live and do not add route handlers, hosted backend behavior, live probes, downloads, uploads, local path search, arbitrary URL fetch, or stable API guarantees.

Signed Snapshot Format v0 defines a static/offline snapshot contract and repo seed example only; it does not add production signed releases, real signing keys, executable downloads, a public /snapshots/ route, relay behavior, or native-client runtime.

Relay Surface Design v0 defines future local/LAN bridge policy only; it does not add a relay runtime, protocol server, network listener, private data exposure, live-probe passthrough, or write/admin path.

Native Action / Download / Install Policy v0 defines policy gates for future downloads, mirrors, install handoff, package-manager handoff, execute, restore, uninstall, and rollback actions only; no installer tooling, package-manager integration, malware scanning, rights clearance, executable trust, native clients, or relay runtime is implemented.

Native Local Cache / Privacy Policy v0 defines policy gates for future local cache, private cache, local paths, user state, telemetry, diagnostics, credentials, deletion/export/reset, and portable mode only; no local cache runtime, private file ingestion, local archive scanning, telemetry, accounts, cloud sync, uploads, native clients, or relay runtime is implemented.
Google remains manual-baseline only and is not a live probe source.
Internet Archive live probing remains future, disabled, and gated behind explicit human review.
No live crawling, live source probes, or external source calls are included.
No live source probes are enabled by the wrapper.
No custom domain is configured and no alternate static host is active.
No scraping and no automated Google or Internet Archive baseline collection.
No arbitrary local path access in public-alpha mode.
No auth, accounts, rate limiting, TLS ownership, logging policy, monitoring, or process supervision.
No telemetry, analytics, diagnostics upload, account system, cloud sync, private cache runtime, or private file ingestion.
No CORS, auth, or rate-limit policy is finalized for a public live backend.
No production Rust backend.
No native GUI apps.
No production signed snapshot bundles, public /snapshots/ route, relay services, protocol bridges, or native-client runtime surfaces.
No installer automation, launch automation, package-manager integration, executable download surface, or app-store workflow.
No universal compatibility oracle; compatibility evidence remains source-backed and bounded.
No trust, malware, safety, or reputation scoring.
No rights clearance claim or executable trust claim.
Fixture-backed evidence is not global truth.
External baselines are manual and time-sensitive until human observation records exist.
